title: Budget Implementation Act, 2007
description: Budget Implementation Act, 2007 (Budget Implementation Act, 2007). Current to 2026-02-04.

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: 1 This Act may be cited as the Budget Implementation Act, 2007 .

provisions.chapter: PART 2 Amendments to the Excise Tax Act (Other than with Respect to the Goods and Services Tax/Harmonized Sales Tax)
PART 2 Amendments to the Excise Tax Act (Other than with Respect to the Goods and Services Tax/Harmonized Sales Tax)

provisions.title: Coming into force
provisions.content: 57 Sections 55 and 56 are deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2000.

provisions.title: Payment of $250,000,000
provisions.content: 58 For the fiscal year beginning on April 1, 2007, from and out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, there may be paid, on the requisition of the Minister of Finance, an amount of $250,000,000 to the Province of Ontario, to assist the province in the transition to a single corporate tax administration.

provisions.title: Payment of $150,000,000
provisions.content: 59 For the fiscal year beginning on April 1, 2008, from and out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, there may be paid, on the requisition of the Minister of Finance, an amount of $150,000,000 to the Province of Ontario, to assist the province in the transition to a single corporate tax administration.

provisions.title: Enactment of Act
provisions.content: 60 The Tax-back Guarantee Act is enacted as follows: [See Tax-back Guarantee Act ]
80 [Amendment] Nova Scotia and Newfoundland and Labrador Additional Fiscal Equalization Offset Payments Act

provisions.title: Newfoundland and Labrador
provisions.content: 84 (1) Sections 79 and 82 come into force, or are deemed to have come into force, on April 1 of the first fiscal year in respect of which Newfoundland and Labrador makes the election under subsection 3.7(3) of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act . Footnote * (2) The Minister of Finance shall publish in the Canada Gazette the date on which sections 79 and 82 come into force. Return to footnote * [Note: Sections 79 and 82 in force April 1, 2010, see Canada Gazette Part I, Volume 146, page 26.] (3) Section 81 comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on April 1, 2008. Previous Version

provisions.title: Order in council
provisions.content: 89 This Part comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council. Return to footnote * [Note: Part 7 in force October 26, 2007, see SI/2007-95.]

provisions.chapter: PART 8 Amendments to the Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation Act
provisions.title: Order in council
Order in council
provisions.content: 123 Subsections 91(2), 103(2), 104(2) and 113(2) come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council. Return to footnote * [Note: Subsection 103(2) in force November 17, 2007, see SI/2007-105; subsections 91(2), 104(2) and 113(2) in force November 17, 2007, see SI/2007-106.]

provisions.title: Maximum payment of $30,000,000
provisions.content: 124 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Finance, a sum not exceeding thirty million dollars to the Province of British Columbia to promote fair and equitable economic development, in an environmentally sustainable and ecologically integrated manner, of First Nations in the Spirit Bear Rainforest area of British Columbia and the Queen Charlotte Islands of British Columbia.

provisions.chapter: Clean Air and Climate Change Trust Fund
provisions.title: Maximum payment of $1,519,000,000
provisions.content: 125 (1) The Minister of Finance may make direct payments, in an aggregate amount not exceeding one billion, five hundred and nineteen million dollars, to a trust established to provide provinces and territories with funding to support provincial and territorial projects that will result in reductions in greenhouse gas emissions and air pollutants. Territories may also use those funds to adapt to a changing climate. (2) The amount that may be provided to a province or territory under this section is to be determined in accordance with the terms of the trust indenture establishing the trust referred to in subsection (1). (3) Any amount payable under this section may be paid by the Minister of Finance out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund at the times and in the manner that the Minister of Finance considers appropriate.

provisions.title: Maximum payment of $614,100,000
provisions.content: 126 (1) The Minister of Finance may make direct payments, in an aggregate amount not exceeding six hundred and fourteen million, one hundred thousand dollars, to a trust established to provide the Province of Ontario with funding for post-secondary education and training and the Provinces of Manitoba and Saskatchewan with funding for training. (2) The amount that may be provided to a province under this section is to be determined in accordance with the terms of the trust indenture establishing the trust referred to in subsection (1) and allocated as follows: (a) a sum not exceeding five hundred and seventy-four million dollars to the Province of Ontario; (b) a sum not exceeding twenty-one million, seven hundred thousand dollars to the Province of Manitoba; and (c) a sum not exceeding eighteen million, four hundred thousand dollars to the Province of Saskatchewan. (3) Any amount payable under this section may be paid by the Minister of Finance out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund at the times and in the manner that the Minister of Finance considers appropriate.

provisions.title: Maximum payment of $300,000,000
provisions.content: 127 (1) The Minister of Finance may make direct payments, in an aggregate amount not exceeding three hundred million dollars, to a trust established to provide the provinces and territories with funding in support of human papillomavirus immunization. (2) The amount that may be provided to a province or territory under this section is to be determined in accordance with the terms of the trust indenture establishing the trust referred to in subsection (1). (3) Any amount payable under this section may be paid by the Minister of Finance out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund at the times and in the manner that the Minister of Finance considers appropriate.

provisions.title: Maximum payment of $612,000,000
provisions.content: 128 (1) The Minister of Finance may make direct payments, in an aggregate amount not exceeding six hundred and twelve million dollars, to a trust established to provide provinces and territories with funding in support of a patient wait times guarantee. (2) The amount that may be provided to a province or territory under this section is to be determined in accordance with the terms of the trust indenture establishing the trust referred to in subsection (1). (3) Any amount payable under this section may be paid by the Minister of Finance out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund at the times and in the manner that the Minister of Finance considers appropriate.

provisions.title: Maximum payment of $250,000,000
provisions.content: 129 (1) The Minister of Finance may, for the fiscal year beginning on April 1, 2007, make direct payments, in an aggregate amount not exceeding two hundred and fifty million dollars to the provinces and territories for the purpose of supporting the creation of child care spaces. (2) The amount that may be paid to a province or territory for the fiscal year referred to in subsection (1) is the amount determined by multiplying the amount set out in that subsection by the quotient obtained by dividing (a) the population of the province or territory for the fiscal year by (b) the total of the population of all provinces and territories for the fiscal year. (3) Any amount payable under this section may be paid by the Minister of Finance out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund at the times and in the manner that the Minister of Finance considers appropriate.

provisions.title: Payment of $3,500,000
provisions.content: 130 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Finance, the sum of three million, five hundred thousand dollars to Yukon.

provisions.title: Payment of $54,400,000
provisions.content: 131 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Finance, the sum of fifty-four million, four hundred thousand dollars to the Northwest Territories.

provisions.title: Maximum payment of $225,000,000
provisions.content: 132 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of the Environment, a sum not exceeding two hundred and twenty-five million dollars to the Nature Conservancy of Canada for its use.

provisions.title: Maximum payment of $400,000,000
provisions.content: 133 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Health, a sum not exceeding four hundred million dollars to Canada Health Infoway Inc. for its use.

provisions.title: Maximum payment of $96,000,000
provisions.content: 134 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Industry, a sum not exceeding ninety-six million dollars to CANARIE Inc. for its use.

provisions.title: Maximum payment of $100,000,000
provisions.content: 135 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Industry, a sum not exceeding one hundred million dollars to Genome Canada for its use.

provisions.title: Afghanistan Reconstruction Trust Fund — $90,000,000
provisions.content: 136 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister for International Development, a sum not exceeding ninety million dollars to the World Bank for the Afghanistan Reconstruction Trust Fund in respect of development assistance for Afghanistan. Previous Version

provisions.title: UN Mine Action Service — $20,000,000
provisions.content: 137 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister for International Development, a sum not exceeding twenty million dollars to the United Nations for use in UN Mine Action Service activities in Afghanistan. Previous Version

provisions.title: UN Office on Drugs and Crime — $13,000,000
provisions.content: 138 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, a sum not exceeding thirteen million dollars to the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime, in respect of counter-narcotics initiatives for Afghanistan.

provisions.title: Counter Narcotics Trust Fund — $2,000,000
provisions.content: 139 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, a sum not exceeding two million dollars to the United Nations Development Programme for the Counter Narcotics Trust Fund, in respect of counter-narcotics initiatives for Afghanistan.

provisions.title: Law and Order Trust Fund for Afghanistan — $10,000,000
provisions.content: 140 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, a sum not exceeding ten million dollars to the United Nations Development Programme for the Law and Order Trust Fund for Afghanistan, for use consistent with the purposes of that fund.

provisions.chapter: Rick Hansen Man in Motion Foundation
provisions.title: Maximum payment of $30,000,000
provisions.content: 141 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Health, a sum not exceeding thirty million dollars to the Rick Hansen Man in Motion Foundation for its use.

provisions.chapter: The Perimeter Institute for Theoretical Physics
provisions.title: Maximum payment of $50,000,000
provisions.content: 142 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Industry, a sum not exceeding fifty million dollars to The Perimeter Institute for Theoretical Physics for its use.

provisions.chapter: Canada Foundation for Sustainable Development Technology
provisions.title: Maximum payment of $200,000,000
provisions.content: 143 There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Industry, a sum not exceeding $200 million to the Canada Foundation for Sustainable Development Technology for its use. Previous Version

provisions.chapter: Canada Foundation for Sustainable Development Technology
provisions.title: Clarification — maximum payment
provisions.content: 143.1 Despite section 143, the sum that may be paid out under that section is not to exceed the difference between $200 million and the sum paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of the Environment and the Minister of Natural Resources to the Canada Foundation for Sustainable Development Technology for its use, under section 143 as it read immediately before the day on which this section comes into force.
